prompt: What types of coffee beans do you import from Kerala?
response: We specialize in single-origin Arabica and Robusta beans from the Wayanad, Coorg, and Chikmagalur regions of Kerala and neighboring South Indian areas. Our most popular varieties are the Monsooned Malabar Arabica (known for its low acidity and bold, spicy notes) and the high-altitude Robusta from the Nilgiri Hills (full-bodied with a chocolatey finish). All beans are shade-grown and handpicked.

prompt: I want to order a classic South Indian filter coffee for home delivery. How does it work?
response: You can order our signature 'Classic Madras Filter Coffee' through our website or app. We deliver the freshly brewed coffee in our insulated, sustainable packaging within 30 minutes in the city. The coffee is made with our special 80:20 Robusta-Arabica blend, freshly decocted with jaggery notes. You can also subscribe for daily morning delivery!

prompt: Tell me about your coffee bean processing method.
response: We use a traditional 'sun-drying and monsooning' process for our Malabar beans. After handpicking, the beans are sun-dried for 2 weeks, then exposed to the monsoon winds in ventilated warehouses for 3-4 months. This unique process reduces acidity and gives the beans their distinctive bluish-green hue and bold, earthy flavor. Our other beans are processed using the washed (wet) method to preserve their inherent floral and fruity notes.

prompt: Do you offer any espresso blends?
response: Yes! Our 'God's Own Espresso Blend' is a bestseller. It combines 70% Robusta from Wayanad (for the rich crema and caffeine kick) with 30% Arabica from Chikmagalur (for acidity and complexity). It's roasted medium-dark, perfect for a strong South Indian-style espresso or a velvety cappuccino. We also have a single-origin Arabica light roast for pour-over enthusiasts.

prompt: What makes your coffee beans 'high quality'?
response: Our quality is ensured through: 1) Direct trade with certified organic estates in Kerala. 2) Handpicking only ripe cherries (Grade A). 3) Traditional processing monitored by our experts. 4) Small-batch roasting in our facility to ensure peak freshness. 5) Every batch is cupped and scored above 85 points on the SCA scale. We provide traceability details with every pack.

prompt: Can I buy unroasted green coffee beans?
response: Absolutely. We sell green beans from our 'Reserve Collection' for home roasters. Currently, we have unroasted Monsooned Malabar AA, Nilgiri Washed Robusta, and Pulney Hills Arabica. Each comes with detailed processing notes and recommended roast profiles. Available in 1kg and 5kg packs.

prompt: What's on your menu for iced coffees?
response: Our summer menu features: 1) **Kerala Cold Brew** - 18-hour steeped Nilgiri Robusta with coconut palm sugar. 2) **Filter Coffee Frappé** - Blended classic filter coffee with vanilla ice cream. 3) **Spiced Mocha Iced Coffee** - Our dark roast with cocoa, cardamom, and chili. 4) **Iced Pour-Over** - Single-origin light roast served over a large ice cube. All available for delivery.

prompt: Do you have a coffee subscription service?
response: Yes! Our 'Kerala Coffee Club' offers three subscriptions: 1) **Bean of the Month**: 500g of a new single-origin or blend each month. 2) **Daily Fix**: Weekly delivery of 250g of your chosen grind. 3) **Brewer's Box**: Includes beans, a seasonal spice blend (like ginger-cardamom), and brewing guide. Subscribers get 15% off, free delivery, and first access to new harvests.

prompt: How should I store your coffee beans to keep them fresh?
response: For maximum freshness: 1) Store in an airtight, opaque container. 2) Keep in a cool, dark place (not the fridge or freezer unless for long-term storage). 3) Grind only just before brewing. 4) Consume within 2-3 weeks of opening for peak flavor. Our bags have a one-way degassing valve, so you can keep them sealed until first use.

prompt: Do you cater for corporate events or offices?
response: We provide comprehensive corporate solutions: 1) **Office Coffee Service**: Bean delivery, equipment rental (espresso machines, filter brewers), and barista training. 2) **Event Catering**: Pop-up South Indian filter coffee stalls with live brewing. 3) **Custom Branding**: Coffee bags with your company logo for gifting. We've served several tech parks in the city with our 'Sustainable Sips' program.

prompt: Are your coffee beans ethically sourced?
response: Ethical sourcing is our cornerstone. We: 1) Practice **Direct Trade** - paying 40% above Fair Trade prices directly to estate workers' cooperatives. 2) Fund **Educational Initiatives** in the Wayanad region. 3) Are **Certified Carbon Neutral** in our shipping. 4) Support **Women-led coffee cooperatives** in Kerala. Our annual impact report is available on our website.

prompt: I'm new to brewing. What equipment do you recommend for your beans?
response: For beginners: 1) **South Indian Filter** (our heritage method) for a strong, traditional decoction. 2) **French Press** for our medium-roast blends to highlight body. 3) **Aeropress** for our light-roast single-origins for clarity. We sell starter kits on our website and offer free online brewing workshops every Saturday.

prompt: What's your best-selling coffee drink?
response: Our **'Madras Kaapi'** is the crowd favorite. It's our traditional filter coffee made with the 80:20 blend, served in a stainless steel davara-tumbler set. The coffee is frothed by pouring it back and forth between the vessels, creating the perfect temperature and creamy froth (called 'meter coffee'). It accounts for 60% of our shop and delivery sales.

prompt: Do you sell any merchandise?
response: We sell a range of coffee-related merchandise: 1) Traditional stainless steel **davara-tumbler sets**. 2) Handcrafted **brass coffee filters**. 3) **Organic cotton aprons** with block prints of coffee cherries. 4) **Custom ceramic mugs** made by local artisans. 5) Reusable **tumbler cups** for discounts on takeaway orders. Available online and in-store.

prompt: My delivery coffee arrived lukewarm. What can you do?
response: We sincerely apologize. Our insulated packaging is designed to keep coffee hot for 45 minutes. Please contact our customer care at care@keralacoffee.com or call +91-XXX-XXXX within 15 minutes of delivery. We will immediately send a free replacement and offer a 50% discount on your next order. We also track delivery temperatures to improve our process.

prompt: Can I get a custom blend for my cafe?
response: Certainly! Our 'Create Your Blend' service is popular with cafes. Our master roaster will work with you on: 1) **Profile Tasting** to understand your preference. 2) **Blending Trials** (up to 5 variants). 3) **Private Labeling** with your cafe's name and roast date. 4) **Bulk Supply Contracts** (minimum 10kg per month). We currently supply to 12 boutique cafes in the city.

prompt: What is the difference between your 'Monsooned Malabar' and regular coffee?
response: Monsooned Malabar undergoes a unique 4-month process where beans are exposed to moist monsoon winds. This causes them to swell, lose acidity, and develop a **distinctly bold, spicy, and earthy profile** with a syrupy body. Regular Arabica beans are brighter and more acidic. It's an acquired taste, perfect for those who love strong, non-acidic coffee, and is a cornerstone of Italian espresso blends.

prompt: Do you offer decaffeinated coffee?
response: Yes, we offer a Swiss Water Process decaf version of our Nilgiri Arabica beans. It's 99.9% caffeine-free without chemical solvents, preserving the bean's original chocolate and nutty notes. Available as whole beans (medium roast) and pre-ground for filter coffee. We also have a decaf option for our 'God's Own Espresso Blend'.

prompt: What are your shop timings?
response: Our flagship store is open from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM, seven days a week. The morning hours (7-10 AM) feature a 'Sunrise Special' with discounted filter coffee and fresh banana bread. Home delivery is available from 7:00 AM to 10:30 PM via our app or website. You can also schedule pre-dawn deliveries for as early as 5:30 AM.

prompt: I received my beans but the bag looks puffy. Is it safe?
response: That's perfectly normal and indicates freshness! Our bags have a **one-way degassing valve**. After roasting, coffee beans release carbon dioxide for weeks. The valve lets CO2 escape without letting oxygen in, preventing the bag from bursting. This puffiness means your beans are freshly roasted and actively degassing. Just press the gas out gently before sealing.

prompt: Do you have any vegan options on your menu?
response: All our black coffees and espresso shots are vegan. For milk-based drinks, we offer **house-made vegan alternatives**: Coconut-cashew 'Mylk', Oat milk, and Almond milk (all unsweetened). Our plant-based 'Coconut Cold Brew' and 'Vegan Kaapi' (with coconut mylk foam) are very popular. All pastries are marked clearly, and we have 3 vegan snack options daily.

prompt: Can I visit the estates in Kerala that you source from?
response: We organize annual **'Origin Trips'** for our Kerala Coffee Club members! It's a 4-day curated experience visiting our partner estates in Wayanad, participating in cherry picking, processing workshops, and cupping sessions led by our master roaster. Please express your interest via our website to be notified about the next trip. We also have virtual estate tours online.

prompt: What's the grind size for a South Indian filter?
response: For the traditional stainless steel filter, you need a **fine grind, but not as fine as espresso**. Think the consistency of coarse sand or table salt. If you order our beans pre-ground, select the '**South Indian Filter**' option. Using a grind that's too fine will clog the filter, and too coarse will result in a weak decoction. We have a detailed video guide on our website.

prompt: My order hasn't arrived yet. What's the status?
response: I apologize for the delay. Please provide your order number, and I can check the live GPS status of our delivery executive. Typically, deliveries take 25-35 minutes. If it's beyond 45 minutes, your next coffee is on us. You can also track your order in real-time via the link in your confirmation SMS. For immediate assistance, call our dispatch manager at +91-XXX-XXXX.
